app.author: Author: {author}
conns.scope_note: All captured connections (per "Traffic to modify") - process/IP/port targeting decides what gets impaired, not what gets listed.
conns.totals: Sum: down {down} KB, up {up} KB, total {total} KB
dialogs.buffer_help:
The buffer only matters when you set a download/upload limit. It's a queue where packets wait when they arrive faster than the limit lets through.

Big buffer = long queue: packets wait longer (more delay), but almost nothing is lost.
Small buffer = short queue: little delay, but once it fills, packets start getting dropped.

Important: the speed you set is always delivered. The buffer only changes one thing - delay vs. loss.

What to set:
  • You just want to cap the speed, delay doesn't matter → 1000-2000 ms.
  • You want to fake a slow, laggy link → enter how many ms of lag you want (e.g. 300 = slight, 2000 = very laggy).
  • You want to see packets being dropped → 100-300 ms.

Watch the test length:
  • Short test (a few seconds): with a big buffer you may see no loss - the queue hasn't filled yet. Use a smaller buffer or test longer.
  • Longer test: loss shows up once the queue fills.

0 = endless queue: nothing is dropped, but delay can grow forever. Usually not needed.

Default: 1000 ms.
dialogs.confirm_close_running: The simulation is still running. Stop it and close the application?
dialogs.match_help:
An expression is a comma-separated list of terms; positives are OR-ed, "!" subtracts.

  value          a single value (a bare process name matches as a substring)
  a, b, c        a list
  a-b            a range, both ends included (80-88 = 80 through 88)
  >N  <N  >=N  <=N   comparisons (numbers only)
  *  ?           wildcards (chrome*, fire?ox)
  re:PATTERN     a regular expression (case-insensitive; escape a comma as \,)
  10.0.0.0/24    CIDR (IP fields only; IPv4 and IPv6)
  !value         exclude

Examples:
  chrome.exe, !chromedriver
  80, 443, 8000-8100
  10.0.0.1-10.0.0.50, !10.0.0.7
  re:^fire

An empty field means "everything". A field with only exclusions means "everything except those". A term that cannot be evaluated for a packet (a port rule against ICMP, an IPv4 rule against an IPv6 address) simply does not match.
dialogs.profile_name_taken: This name is already used by a built-in preset. Choose another name.
dialogs.profile_scope_warning:
A profile stores only the link characteristics: loss, corruption, duplication, latency, jitter and speed limits.

These active settings will NOT be saved in the profile: {fields}.

Use 'Save file...' to store the complete configuration.
dialogs.reset_layout_body: Forget the remembered window size and position, collapsed sections and table sorting? Your settings and the current session are kept.
errors.bad_config_value: Invalid value for '{field}' in the config file: {value}
errors.bad_filter_bounds: Field '{field}': '{term}' is out of the allowed range ({min}-{max}).
errors.bad_filter_compare: Field '{field}': comparison '{term}' needs a number after the operator.
errors.bad_filter_compare_name: Field '{field}': comparison operators (>, <, >=, <=) work only with a PID (a number), not a process name - '{term}'.
errors.bad_filter_ip: Field '{field}': '{term}' is not a valid IP address, range, CIDR, wildcard or re: pattern.
errors.bad_filter_ip_family: Field '{field}': range '{term}' mixes IPv4 and IPv6 - both ends must use the same address family.
errors.bad_filter_number: Field '{field}': '{term}' is not a number, range, wildcard or re: pattern.
errors.bad_filter_range: Field '{field}': range '{term}' is reversed - the start must not be greater than the end.
errors.bad_filter_regex: Field '{field}': '{term}' is not a valid regular expression (a comma inside a pattern must be escaped: \,).
errors.field_number: The '{name}' field must be a number.
errors.scenario_empty: Scenario: the step list is empty.
errors.scenario_not_a_scenario: This file is not a scenario (expected a list of steps, or an object with "steps").
errors.scenario_step_at: Scenario, step {step}: "at" must be a number of seconds (>= 0).
errors.scenario_step_empty: Scenario, step {step}: it does nothing (no "settings" and no "action").
errors.scenario_step_settings: Scenario, step {step}: "settings" must be an object.
errors.seed_integer: The 'Seed' field must be an integer.
events.queue_overflow: Latency queue overflowed - the tool dropped packets of its own
fields.lan_mode: LAN mode (local network only, no internet)
fields.locked_running: Locked while a session runs (STOP unlocks it).
fields.schedule_overrides: The throughput schedule is active - it replaces these limits (the rates come from its steps).
fields.target_no_match: No running process matches this target - traffic is NOT being impaired.
frames.target_process: Target process  (name, PID, list, wildcard, re: ...; empty = all traffic)
log.apply_needed: Click "Apply changes" to push this to the running session.
log.csv_rotated: CSV column set changed - previous statistics moved to
log.dest_set: Destination set from the connection table
log.duration_reached: Time limit reached ({v} s) - session stopped.
log.engine_fault: Engine fault: {e} - the session was stopped, your network is back to normal.
log.no_process_for_row: No process name for that row (start as Administrator to resolve names).
log.no_pydivert: No pydivert. Install:  pip install pydivert
log.note_windows: NOTE: capture works only on Windows (WinDivert).
log.queue_overflow: WARNING: the latency queue is full ({q} packets) - the TOOL is now dropping packets you did not ask to lose ({n} so far). Your measured loss is not your application's. Lower the latency, or the throughput you are pushing through it.
log.ready: Ready. Set parameters and click START.
log.reset_needs_start: TCP reset works only while a session is running - press START first.
log.resetting: Resetting active TCP connections (RST) for ~3 s...
log.start_first: Start first (START) to create session data.
log.target_set: Target process set from the connection table
log.targeting_none: Targeting: NO matching process - traffic is NOT being impaired.
log.targeting_requires_psutil: Targeting requires psutil:  pip install psutil
log.ui_error: Internal error: {e} (the session keeps running; see the log).
menu.open_event_log: Open event log in a window
prefs.chart_seconds_hint: how far back the graph goes
prefs.confirm_close: Ask before closing while a capture is running
prefs.restore_profile: Restore the last profile on startup
stats.scope_note: Counters cover ALL captured traffic (per "Traffic to modify"), not only the traffic your targeting impairs.
tips.about: Version, author, licence and the third-party components this program ships with.
tips.apply: Applies setting changes WITHOUT stopping (live). The traffic filter can only be changed by restarting.
tips.avg_rate: Average throughput since start = total MB / duration.
tips.block: Block (drop) all traffic to matching destinations. IP and Port each take a comma-separated list; blocking triggers on IP OR Port (an empty field is ignored). Supports ranges, CIDR, wildcards and ! to exclude. Other traffic is unaffected.
tips.buffer: Link buffer for the speed limit, in ms: how much queueing delay a rate-limited link may build up before it starts dropping (bufferbloat). 0 = unlimited buffer. Only matters when a download/upload limit or a schedule is set.
tips.chart_seconds: How many seconds of throughput history the chart keeps. Longer shows more of the past; shorter zooms in on the last moments.
tips.col_avg: Average packet size on this connection (total bytes / packets), in bytes.
tips.col_down: Data received (downloaded) on this connection, in kilobytes.
tips.col_dropped: Packets dropped on this connection by the active impairments (loss, link outage, LAN mode, resets, ...).
tips.col_dur: Seconds between the first and the last packet of this connection in the current session.
tips.col_event_desc: What happened: the settings that were applied, or your bug marker.
tips.col_event_type: START / STOP / CHANGE / SCENARIO / BUG / RESET.
tips.col_idle: Seconds since the last packet. It stops counting when the session stops.
tips.col_kb: Total data on this connection (download + upload), in kilobytes.
tips.col_local_port: Port on this machine. It is what links the connection to a process.
tips.col_packets: Packets seen on this connection since it appeared.
tips.col_pid: Process id owning the local port, resolved when the packet was captured.
tips.col_process: The process that owns the local port. '?' means the name could not be resolved - run as Administrator.
tips.col_proto: Transport protocol of the connection (TCP / UDP / IP).
tips.col_remote_ip: Address this machine is talking to. Right-click a row to limit the impairments to it.
tips.col_remote_port: Port on the remote side (443 = HTTPS, 53 = DNS, 80 = HTTP).
tips.col_scoped: Whether this connection is in targeting scope - i.e. being impaired, not merely observed. With no targeting set, every connection is in scope.
tips.col_up: Data sent (uploaded) on this connection, in kilobytes.
tips.confirm_close: When on, closing the window during a running capture asks for confirmation first. Turn off to close without being asked.
tips.conn_search: Filter connections by IP address, port or direction (e.g. '443', '10.0.0', 'out').
tips.conn_table: Who (process) talks to whom (IP:port). 'time[s]' is the connection duration, 'idle[s]' is seconds since the last packet. Click a header to sort; the list scrolls vertically and horizontally.
tips.copy_cli: Copies to the clipboard a ready CLI command that reproduces these conditions (a frozen .exe uses its own name).
tips.corrupt: Percent of packets with one flipped data bit - tests resilience to corrupted data.
tips.data_down: How much data actually passed downstream (download) since start - real usage. Hovering also shows how much the app tried to download.
tips.data_total: Sum of downloaded and uploaded data this session.
tips.data_up: How much data actually passed upstream (upload) since start.
tips.delete_profile: Delete the selected profile. Built-in presets cannot be deleted.
tips.dest: Impair only traffic to/from chosen servers. IP and Port each take a comma-separated list; setting both narrows to traffic matching IP AND port (an empty field = any). Prefix a term with ! to exclude it. See '?' for the full syntax. Other traffic passes unchanged.
tips.donate: Support the project (voluntary). Opens donislawdev.com/support in your browser - it funds further development.
tips.down_limit: Max throughput of INCOMING traffic (download) in KB/s. 0 = unlimited. Note: ping uses tiny packets, so a speed limit barely affects it.
tips.dup: Percent of packets sent twice - a real symptom of a poor network.
tips.duration: Stop the session automatically after this many seconds. 0 = run until you press STOP. Applied at START only (like the traffic filter).
tips.eff_seed: The exact seed used in this session. Put it in the Seed field and run again to get the same randomization - the key to reproducing a bug.
tips.event_copy: Copies the selected rows (with their headers) to the clipboard, tab-separated - paste straight into a bug report.
tips.event_search: Filter the events by direction, kind or description (e.g. 'bug', 'reset', 'out').
tips.event_sort: Click a column header to sort the log (click again to reverse order).
tips.export_conns: Save the current connection list (with your search and sort) to a CSV file (bean_network_tester_connections.csv), download/upload split into separate byte columns.
tips.export_csv: Append the current counters to a CSV file (bean_network_tester_stats.csv) - for a report or analysis.
tips.filter: Which traffic to modify. 'Both directions' covers TCP, UDP and ping (ICMP); the other options narrow it down. For a single port or address use 'Target destination' below. Locked while running (STOP to change).
tips.flap: Cyclic full outages: every 'Period' seconds the link is dead for the given percent of the time. Simulates a flapping connection (e.g. weak WiFi).
tips.freeze: Freeze the table so rows stop moving while you inspect or copy them.
tips.jitter: Random delay variation (+/- ms). Ping starts to jump instead of being steady; also lightly reorders packets.
tips.lan_mode: Simulates a network with no internet access: traffic to/from public addresses is dropped, while the local network (LAN: 10.x, 192.168.x, 172.16-31.x, loopback) works. Tests how the app behaves when the internet is down but the intranet is up.
tips.language: Interface language. Switching rebuilds the UI but keeps the current session and settings. Locked while running.
tips.latency: How many milliseconds of delay to add to every packet - this raises ping. E.g. 100 ms = noticeably slower responses.
tips.load_config: Load all settings from a JSON file (same format as CLI: --config).
tips.log_lines: How many log lines to keep in the strip at the bottom. Older lines are dropped once the limit is reached.
tips.loss: Percent of packets that vanish without a trace. Even 5% feels like a dropping connection.
tips.mark_bug: Inserts a timestamped marker into the log - click exactly when you see the bug.
tips.match_syntax:
Filter syntax (the same in every field):
  80,443        a list - any of them
  1000-2000     a range, both ends included
  >1024  <=80   comparisons: > < >= <=
  !53           exclusion - everything except 53
  8*  chrome*   wildcard (* and ?)
  re:^chrome    regular expression
IP also takes CIDR (192.168.1.0/24) and IPv6.
Terms are combined: any positive term matches, every ! term excludes. A field with only exclusions means 'everything except those'. Empty = everything.
tips.mtu: Drop packets larger than the given number of bytes. Reproduces the 'MTU black hole' common in tunnels/VPN/behind NAT: small packets pass, large ones vanish. 0 = off.
tips.nat: NAT mapping expiry: if a connection is silent longer than the given seconds, the next inbound packet is dropped (the mapping 'disappears'). Tests keep-alive. 0 = off.
tips.peak_rate: The highest throughput reached, averaged over a 1 s window (a speed limit shapes the average rate, so a shorter window would report bursts above the limit).
tips.profiles: Ready-made settings (presets, e.g. '3G network') and your own. Selecting one fills the fields above immediately.
tips.reset_layout: Forget the remembered window size and position, collapsed sections and table sorting, and recentre the window. Your settings and session are kept.
tips.reset_now: Reset all active TCP connections now (for ~3 s). UDP has no reset - use loss or a link outage for it. Works after START.
tips.restore_profile: When on, the tool reopens with the last profile you picked already filled in. It does not start a capture - you still press START.
tips.row_limit: The most rows a table will show. The tables render only what is on screen, so a large limit costs nothing to draw - it only bounds the filtering and sorting work. 0 = show everything.
tips.rst: Chance that a TCP packet resets its own connection (an injected RST). TCP only - UDP has no reset.
tips.rst_cooldown: How long a connection stays reset (blocked) after an RST, in seconds.
tips.save_config: Save all current settings to a JSON file to reproduce them or use in the CLI.
tips.save_profile: Save the current link characteristics (loss, corruption, duplication, latency, jitter, speed limits) as your own profile. The remaining settings are stored via 'Save file...'.
tips.save_repro: Saves a JSON file with everything needed to reproduce: seed, settings, counters, event log, connections and a ready CLI command.
tips.scenario: A JSON scenario file changes settings in real time (e.g. after 10 s add ping, after 20 s drop the link). 'Loop' replays it over and over.
tips.schedule: Variable throughput over time. Format: time:download:upload in KB/s, comma-separated. E.g. '2:100:0, 2:500:0' = 2 s at 100 KB/s, then 2 s at 500, looping.
tips.scope_note: The traffic filter decides what is captured. Target process / destination IP / port only decide which of those packets get impaired - everything else is passed through untouched, but still counted and listed.
tips.seed: Random seed. Set any number so every run randomizes identically - this makes a bug reproducible. Empty = different every time.
tips.settings: App settings: interface language and how many rows the tables show.
tips.spike: Occasional ping spikes: with the given probability (%) add extra delay (ms) to a single packet. Reproduces momentary 'lag'.
tips.start: Turns traffic modification on/off. Picking a preset or changing fields does nothing on its own - only START begins impairing. Requires running as administrator.
tips.stat_block: Packets dropped by a block (firewall) rule.
tips.stat_corrupted: Packets whose payload had a data bit flipped. Packets with no payload (e.g. bare ACKs) can't be corrupted - they pass through and aren't counted here, so this can be below the set percentage.
tips.stat_flap: Packets dropped by a link outage (flapping). Counted separately from loss.
tips.stat_lan: Packets to/from the internet dropped in LAN mode.
tips.stat_loss: Packets dropped because of the configured Loss (or link outages).
tips.stat_mtu: Packets dropped as too large (MTU black hole).
tips.stat_nat: Packets dropped after the NAT mapping expired.
tips.stat_overflow: Packets dropped because the queue overflowed (heavy overload).
tips.stat_queue: How many packets are waiting in the queue (grows with latency or a speed limit).
tips.stat_rate: Packets dropped because the speed-limit buffer filled up (offered traffic stayed above the limit longer than the buffer holds). Counted separately from loss and from tool overflow.
tips.stat_rst_sent: How many RST packets were injected to reset connections.
tips.stat_seen: How many packets passed through the tool in total.
tips.stat_shutdown: Packets that were still waiting in the delay queue when you pressed STOP. They were not lost in the network - the session simply ended before they were released.
tips.status: Whether traffic modification is running. Green = running, red = stopped.
tips.summary: What the tool is doing with your traffic right now. 'Preview' = what START will apply; 'Active' = applied now; 'Not applied yet' = click Apply changes.
tips.syn: Percent of dropped TCP SYN packets, i.e. those starting a connection. Simulates a connection that won't establish - tests retry logic.
tips.target_process: Limit the effect to chosen apps: process names, PIDs, ranges, wildcards or re: patterns (comma-separated). Prefix ! to exclude, empty = all traffic. See '?' for the full syntax. Requires psutil.
tips.up_limit: Max throughput of OUTGOING traffic (upload) in KB/s. 0 = unlimited.
warn.not_admin: Not running as administrator - the WinDivert driver will not load and START will fail. Restart the app as administrator.
warn.queue_overflow: The latency queue is full: the tool is dropping packets you did not ask to lose. The loss you are measuring is partly ours. Lower the latency or the traffic rate.
